pageCaption.value: Dora Nipp’s extensive oral history interviews documenting the lives and experiences of Canada’s immigrants celebrate their ethnic diversity and explore the contribution they have made to their new homeland.

metadescription.value: Dora Nipp’s extensive oral history interviews documenting the lives and experiences of Canada’s immigrants celebrate their ethnic diversity and explore the contribution they have made to their new homeland.

text.value: Dora Nipp’s extensive oral history interviews documenting the lives and experiences of Canada’s immigrants celebrate their ethnic diversity and explore the contribution they have made to their new homeland.

Dora Nipp wants her fellow Canadians to remember and treasure their past, particularly the wide ethnic diversity of the nation’s immigrants. She set out to preserve a collection of oral history interviews with immigrants, recording the joys and hardships of their journeys to, and lives in, Canada. She helped establish a museum in Toronto where visitors can listen to these recordings. As Chief Executive Officer of the Multicultural History Society of Ontario, she expanded the organization’s acquisitions and its volunteer initiatives exponentially. In the past few years, 300-plus volunteers have engaged with more than 500 oral history testimonies, producing, editing and annotating interview transcripts, and researching and writing biographical notes on narrators. In total, the Society has more than 9,000 hours of oral testimonies and 84,000 photographs – gathered from members of 60 ethnic groups now living in Canada.
The more we celebrate these stories, the better equipped we are to confront discrimination today.

In 2017, Nipp and the Society will be involved in Canada’s sesquicentennial celebrations, marking the 150th anniversary of the Confederation, using the archival collections and primary source material to highlight the country’s ethnic diversity and its debt to immigration.

content.value: Hours of interviews recorded by the Multicultural History Society of Ontario

pageTitle.value: Recreating the emerald green forest - Rolex Awards

pageCaption.value: The long, slow retreat of Brazil’s Atlantic Forest has been reversed thanks to Laury Cullen Jr. who is protecting its unique wildlife and engaging local farmers in conservation.

pageTitle.value: Shooting wildlife with a camera - Rolex Awards

pageCaption.value: With India’s wilderness suffering in the face of explosive economic growth, Shekar Dattatri continues to focus his attention on the urgent need to conserve his country’s remaining natural heritage. His subjects range from the tiger to disappearing coastlines.
